perQueryEvaluations.query: What is the current YES price and implied probability on the 'Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?' event on Kalshi?
quotedExcerpts: Based on the search results from Kalshi as of April 19, 2026, I could not find a market specifically for "Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?"

The search process was partial and reached its limit without locating the target event. The upstream results instead returned unrelated markets, such as one regarding Kim Jong-Un's visit to the U.S. during the Trump term.
If you are looking for this specific event, you might try: Searching for "Zohran Mamdani" or "Mamdani" alone to find related series.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Zero actionable output — no price, no probability, no alternative ticker. Buyer cannot act on anything.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Only a date stamp is provided; no timestamps on any market data because no target data was returned.
scoreExplanations.specificity: No numeric data about the requested market. The only concrete item mentioned is an unrelated Kim Jong-Un market, which is not what the buyer asked about.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: A free LLM without tools could give the same 'try these search terms' suggestions — nothing tool-differentiated delivered.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Does not answer the YES price nor implied probability and does not give the buyer confidence that the market does or does not exist on Kalshi.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: No data to evaluate — neutral score.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: Only one tool call issued (search_markets) despite 24 methods being available; never tried get_event_by_slug, resolve_slug, or a broader no-category search.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner locked search to category=Politics and maxSeriesScan=200, which is too narrow for a low-frequency long-dated candidate market.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: executionMs=99754ms on a single search_markets call — suggests the search exhausted the series budget before finding anything useful, yet no follow-up call was issued.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: diagnostics.completeness.evaluations is empty: the completeness stage never ran a needsDifferentTools repair despite an obviously incomplete answer.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: Single toolCall: search_markets args={category:'Politics', query:'Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?', maxSeriesScan:200}. Result returned an unrelated KXUSAKIM-29 top hit, but the loop terminated after one try instead of falling back to resolve_slug or a broader search.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory has exactly 1 entry and summary.loopCount=2 — the runtime observed irrelevant top hits but did not issue a second search with relaxed category or a slug-resolution call.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Honest about failure but totally unhelpful — buyer gets no YES price and no confirmation of whether the market even exists.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: After a search_markets call returns zero title-matched results, auto-retry with category omitted and call resolve_slug / get_event_by_slug on plausible slug guesses before synthesizing a refusal.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: resolve_slug or get_event_by_slug (with slug guesses like 'kxmamdani', 'kxzohranpres'), then search_markets with no category filter
diagnosticBrief.argsUsed: {"limit":10,"query":"Will Zohran Mamdani become President of the United States before 2045?","status":"open","category":"Politics","maxSeriesScan":200}
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: {"query":"Mamdani","status":"open","maxSeriesScan":1500} (no category, keyword reduced to last-name only) and/or resolve_slug with candidate slug
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: toolCallHistory[0].result.results[0].title === "Will Kim Jong-Un visit the United States during Trump's term?" — clearly unrelated; loop terminated anyway.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: {"results":[{"title":"Will Kim Jong-Un visit the United States during Trump's term?","ticker":"KXUSAKIM-29",...}] — no Mamdani-matched series at all.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No evaluations recorded; completeness stage effectively skipped despite an empty-hit answer.
diagnosticBrief.stageTimingBottleneck: executionMs=99754ms (nearly the full async budget spent on one exhausted search_markets call)

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: No — buyer paid and got an admission of failure with no data.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Find all active Kalshi markets in the Elections category with YES implied probability between 10% and 40% and 24h volume above $10,000, ranked by 24h volume.
quotedExcerpts: Based on current tool results, there are no active Kalshi markets in the Elections category that meet both the 10%–40% YES probability range and the 24h volume threshold of $10,000.
Will legislation requiring proof of U.S. citizenship to vote become law before Jan 1, 2027? | KXELECTIONBILL-27JAN01 | 21 (21%) | $3,693.56
The "Elections" category was not directly available for browsing; results were derived from broader "Politics" and keyword-based searches, which may not be exhaustive.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Buyer learns that no Elections markets meet both thresholds and sees 5 near-misses with explicit volumes, which is directionally useful.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: No explicit timestamp on the data rows; only an implicit 'current' reference. Max 2 per rubric rule 4.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Concrete tickers (KXELECTIONBILL-27JAN01, KXVANCOUVERMAYOR-26OCT17-*), probability percentages, and dollar volumes are provided.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-market filter-based screening is tool-differentiated, but the answer reduces to 'nothing qualifies' which limits the paid-product edge.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Acknowledges that the Elections category could not be browsed directly and used Politics as a proxy — the answer is honest but may have missed qualifying Elections markets.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Values vary across rows (no uniform-value red flag); volumes like $3,693.56 and $525 look plausible for near-threshold markets.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: get_markets_by_probability was called with category='Politics' instead of 'Elections'; probability='coinflip' (35–65%) does not match the requested 10–40% band.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner translated 10–40% YES probability to the 'coinflip' preset (35–65%) which structurally can't answer the question.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: browse_category for 'Politics' returned 0 markets — contributor then fell back to search_markets keyword 'elections'. A category='Elections' direct browse was never attempted.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: diagnostics.completeness.evaluations is empty; the answer's own caveat ('Elections category was not directly available') was never flagged by the completeness stage.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0 args {probability:'coinflip', category:'Politics', minLiquidity:10000} — wrong probability band (35–65% not 10–40%) and wrong category ('Politics' not 'Elections'). tc1 browse_category 'Politics' returned totalCount 0. The 10–40% band was never queried with a probability-appropriate filter.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory[0].args.probability='coinflip' and category='Politics' while the user asked for Elections at 10–40% — planner failed to map the user's probability band into the tool's allowed enum and failed to use the correct category.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Buyer gets a mostly honest partial answer with useful near-misses, but the 'no matches' conclusion is suspect because the 10–40% band was never directly filtered on.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Teach the planner to map arbitrary probability bands onto a probability-range argument (or call get_markets_by_probability with a band-appropriate enum) and to prefer category='Elections' directly before falling back to 'Politics'.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_markets_by_probability with category='Elections' and probability band matching 10–40% (e.g. 'long_shot' or similar enum), then paginate through results sorted by volume_24h.
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: tc0 returned warning:'No markets met the requested probability range plus liquidity threshold' — note the range reported was 35-65% not 10-40% → args mismatch.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; completeness check effectively skipped.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: On the fence — the screening logic was miswired, so the 'nothing qualifies' conclusion is unreliable.

perQueryEvaluations.query: For the 'Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?' event (KXNEXTUKPM-30), which candidate sub-market currently has the tightest bid-ask spread relative to its midpoint, and what is that spread in cents?
quotedExcerpts: Based on market data as of April 19, 2026, **Andy Burnham** (KXNEXTUKPM-30-ABUR) has the tightest bid-ask spread relative to its midpoint in the "Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?" event.
The spread for this sub-market is **0.1¢**.
Andy Burnham's sub-market is significantly more liquid than the others, with a relative spread of only 1.24% compared to the next closest candidate, Nigel Farage, at 8.00%.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Directly names the winner (Burnham / KXNEXTUKPM-30-ABUR), reports the spread in cents (0.1¢), and ranks the next 4 candidates with relative spreads — buyer can act immediately.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Date-only ('April 19, 2026') with no time-of-day; no per-row timestamp. Partial freshness signal.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Explicit tickers, bid/ask/spread/midpoint/relative-spread for 5 candidates — maximally specific.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-sub-market ranking with a computed relative-spread metric is not something a free LLM could produce without live orderbook access.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Answers both parts of the question (which candidate, and spread in cents) plus supporting comparison table.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Orderbook values (e.g., ABUR bid 8.0 / ask 8.1) are internally consistent with the raw get_market_orderbook results; spreads cross-check.

traceAssessment.executionIssue: 21 get_market_orderbook calls were issued sequentially to cover all sub-markets — worked, but chewed through ~60% of the async budget on what could have been parallelized.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0 get_event fetched the full nested-market list; tc1..tc20 looped get_market_orderbook per ticker. Functionally correct, but serial fan-out over 20 markets is the main time sink (executionMs=61580).
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: summary.toolCalls=21 with budgetConsumedPct≈61% — execution fan-out is unoptimized but still succeeded; only risk is future timeouts on bigger events.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: High-quality, buyer-ready answer. Only weakness is the serial orderbook fan-out which threatens the budget on larger UK PM-style events.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Parallelize per-ticker orderbook fetches behind a bounded concurrency limit so 20-sub-market screens stay well under budget.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: Same tools, but issued in parallel (or a dedicated 'get_event_orderbooks' batch tool if one existed)
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: Same per-call args, wrapped in Promise.all-style concurrency
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: stageTiming.executionMs=61580 across 21 calls ≈ 2.9s/call serially; under parallel dispatch this would be ~3–5s total.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: KXNEXTUKPM-30-ABUR orderbook: bids[0]={price:8, quantity:32}, asks[0]={price:8.099..., quantity:1} → spread 0.1¢ confirmed.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No repair events; completeness never flagged missing pieces.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Yes — this is a clean paid answer; buyer would pay again.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Using 1-hour candlesticks over the last 7 days, identify the largest single-day YES-price move (in cents) on the 'Will SpaceX land anything successfully on Mars before 2030?' event (KXSPACEXMARS-30).
quotedExcerpts: Based on the 1-hour candlestick data for the last 7 days (as of April 19, 2026), the largest single-day YES-price move for the event **"Will SpaceX land anything successfully on Mars before 2030?" (KXSPACEXMARS-30)** was a **6-cent increase**.
| **Move Magnitude** | 6 cents | | **Direction** | Up (Positive) | | **Price Range** | 26¢ to 32¢ | | **Period Ending (Timestamp)** | 1775941200 |
**Note on Data Fidelity:** This identified move is based on a candlestick stream that contains several null price entries for specific hours.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Names the magnitude (6¢), direction (up), and the ending timestamp — buyer can orient the trade.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Explicit 7-day window plus a per-move unix timestamp (1775941200) — strong freshness signal.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Exact cent move, price range (26–32), and period-ending unix ts.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Rolling-window candlestick aggregation is not a free-LLM capability.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Answers the question and is transparent about null candles; doesn't convert the timestamp to a human date, which is a minor gap.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Internally consistent — range 26→32 = 6c; no red flags. Null handling is disclosed.

traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: Single get_market_candlesticks call with periodInterval=60, 7-day window, includeLatestBeforeStart=true — correctly shaped for the question. Response notes null entries but the call itself did not retry or switch period.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Timestamp 1775941200 left as raw epoch in the response; buyer has to convert to 2026-04-18 herself — minor synthesis polish issue.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Good specific answer with epoch timestamps — the only polish gap is rendering the ts as a human date.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Add a synthesis post-process that converts unix timestamps to ISO or human-friendly dates before rendering.

diagnosticBrief.stageTimingBottleneck: synthesisMs=9771ms (slightly long for a single-call trace)

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Yes — clean paid answer; only nit is the raw unix timestamp.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Screen the Kalshi Economics category for active markets whose orderbook depth can absorb a $5,000 YES order at <= 2 cents of slippage, and list the top 5 by available depth.
quotedExcerpts: Based on data from a recent query of the Kalshi platform, there are currently **no active markets** in the Economics category.
Because the category browse and trending market discovery both returned zero open markets as of April 19, 2026, it was not possible to evaluate order book depth or slippage for a $5,000 YES order.
Consequently, a top 5 list cannot be provided at this time.

scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Only a date; no per-market timestamps because there are no markets.
scoreExplanations.specificity: No tickers, no depths, no prices in the body of the answer.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: A free LLM could state 'no Economics markets active' just as easily.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Does not deliver a top-5 list and does not pivot to an alternative category as a substitute.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: browse_category and discover_trending_markets both returned totalCount=0, then get_all_categories and get_all_series were used to confirm — but analyze_market_liquidity was never called on any Economics series ticker (e.g., KXOFAC, CPIFOOD, RATEHIKE).
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner treated browse_category empty as terminal instead of descending via get_all_series → browse_series → analyze_market_liquidity.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: tc3 get_all_series returned Economics series like KXOFAC, CPIFOOD, RATEHIKE — the runtime had names to drill into but gave up.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: No evaluations recorded; completeness stage didn't flag the missing $5K-depth computation.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc1 discover_trending_markets tradeSnapshot shows allVolume24hZero=true yet tickersWithRecentTrades=244 — there is activity to chase, but the loop didn't pivot to browse_series on the discovered series tickers.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: toolCallHistory tc3 returned Economics series tickers but no follow-up browse_series or analyze_market_liquidity call was made before synthesis.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Honest refusal but buyer gets no partial value; the trace literally had series names to drill into.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When browse_category returns empty, the next iteration should call get_all_series + browse_series on returned tickers before declaring empty.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: browse_series on KXOFAC / CPIFOOD / RATEHIKE followed by analyze_market_liquidity on the resulting tickers
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: tc1.result.tradeSnapshot.tickersWithRecentTrades=244 but tc1.result.totalActive=0 — clear signal to use the trade-fallback path.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: No repair events; needsDifferentTools never fired despite a zero-result top-level answer.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: No — buyer paid for a refusal that the trace itself shows could have been avoided.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Compare the current YES prices and orderbook liquidity across the top 5 candidates in the 'Who will be the next new Speaker of the House?' event (KXNEXTSPEAKER-31) on Kalshi, and identify which candidate's pricing looks most out of line relative to its liquidity.
quotedExcerpts: As of April 19, 2026, 02:25 UTC, the data for the **Who will be the next new Speaker of the House? (KXNEXTSPEAKER-31)** event on Kalshi includes only three candidates.

**Steve Scalise** appears to be the candidate whose pricing is most out of line relative to liquidity for the following reasons: **Widest Spread**: He has a 5.3¢ spread, which is significantly wider than the 3¢ spread maintained by both Hakeem Jeffries and Jim Jordan.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Names the outlier (Scalise) with reasoned evidence — buyer can fade or avoid that contract.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Exact prices, volumes, spreads, and top-of-book price @ qty for 3 candidates.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-candidate liquidity-vs-price comparison is paid-tier analysis.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Top-5 was requested but only 3 candidates exist per upstream — acknowledged honestly; still a partial answer.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Fractional quantities like '12809.22' volume and '377.78' top-ask quantity are unusual for a contract market — not obviously wrong, but a mild red flag given contract units are typically integers.

traceAssessment.completenessIssue: User asked for 'top 5' but upstream returned 3; completeness stage did not attempt a fallback (e.g., search_markets 'Speaker of the House' for additional candidate sub-markets).
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0 get_event returned 3 markets; tc1..tc3 fetched orderbooks for each. Reasoning and output are correct given input, but the 'top 5 vs only 3 available' case was not escalated to a repair step.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Fractional quantities in top-of-book (377.78 contracts) are atypical for prediction-market contract units and may reflect a unit-conversion quirk in the contributor's orderbook normalization.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: User asked for top-5; only 3 returned; diagnostics.completeness.evaluations is empty so no missingParts flag was raised.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Mostly strong answer with one small unit-display oddity and no fallback when top-5 was under-fulfilled.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When requested N exceeds available N, completeness should flag it and trigger a search_markets fan-out on the event title to check for stand-alone sub-markets.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: Also: search_markets(query='Speaker of the House') to confirm no standalone sub-markets exist outside KXNEXTSPEAKER-31.
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: Same plus a follow-up search_markets for 'Speaker House'
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: tc0.result.markets.length === 3 → response should have escalated to search_markets before locking in a 3-row answer.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; 'top-5 vs 3 available' never surfaced as missingParts.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: On the fence — strong outlier call, but the 'top-5 became top-3' gap wasn't probed.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Compare 30-day YES-price trajectories on 'Will OpenAI or Anthropic IPO first?' (KXOAIANTH-40) and 'Will Ramp or Brex IPO first?' (KXRAMPBREX-40). Which event has re-priced more over the last 30 days, in which direction, and by how many cents?
quotedExcerpts: I am unable to determine which event has re-priced more over the last 30 days because historical price data for that period was not available in the tool results.
The candlestick history for these tickers returned empty, so exact start prices from 30 days ago could not be established.
Based on current prices, Anthropic is currently favored over OpenAI (74¢ vs 37¢), and Ramp is strongly favored over Brex (94¢ vs 8¢).

scoreExplanations.actionability: Explicit refusal on the core ask (30-day re-pricing magnitude + direction) — buyer cannot act on the question.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Current prices given without a timestamp; no historical timestamps because history came back empty.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Current YES prices (37¢, 74¢, 94¢, 8¢) and tickers are concrete, but these weren't the ask.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Current prices are on kalshi.com directly; a free user could get these with one click.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Does not answer any of the three sub-asks (which event re-priced more / direction / magnitude).
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Current prices look internally consistent; no red flags on the values shown.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: Initial get_market_candlesticks calls targeted EVENT tickers (KXOAIANTH-40, KXRAMPBREX-40) instead of MARKET tickers — candlesticks require sub-market tickers.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner conflated event ticker with market ticker; only after get_market returned 'not found' did the runtime pivot to get_event_by_slug.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: After discovering sub-market tickers (KXOAIANTH-40-OAI, -ANTH, KXRAMPBREX-40-RAMP, -BREX), get_market_candlesticks STILL returned empty arrays for all of them. That's the upstream gap the auto-weak flag called out.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: Completeness stage did not flag 'no 30-day data → answer unacheivable' and try a different period (e.g., weekly, monthly) or alternative endpoint.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0, tc1, tc8, tc9, tc10 all get_market_candlesticks → candlesticks:[] with periodInterval=1440 (daily) and a 30-day window. Upstream either has no daily candle retention or the start/end ts range (1710892800..1713484800 = March 2024) is off by a year — the query is 'last 30 days' but ts maps to March 2024, not March 2026.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: get_market_candlesticks startTs=1710892800 (2024-03-20) and endTs=1713484800 (2024-04-18) — planner used a 2024 window despite today being 2026-04-19; that's why candlesticks came back empty.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: The orchestrator called this a clarification-gate fix but the real bug is a timestamp arithmetic error: the planner picked a 2024 window, so of course history is empty.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Fix the planner's 30-day window computation to anchor on today's UTC date rather than a fixed 2024 anchor — this single fix would unblock every historical-trajectory query.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_market_candlesticks with startTs ≈ now-30d (~1776000000 for 2026-04) on sub-market tickers
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: startTs 1710892800 decodes to 2024-03-20; endTs 1713484800 decodes to 2024-04-18 — window is 2 years stale.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; completeness did not catch the 2024-vs-2026 timestamp bug.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: No — refusal on the core ask, and the root cause is a stale timestamp window that future queries will keep hitting.

perQueryEvaluations.query: For the 'Who will be named as Xi Jinping's successor?' event (KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01), identify the candidate sub-market that best combines high 24h volume, tight bid-ask spread, and positive 7-day YES-price drift. Which successor is the 'smart-money plus momentum' pick right now?
quotedExcerpts: Based on the data from a past query, the "smart-money plus momentum" pick for the **Xi Jinping successor** event is **Li Qiang**.
| Li Qiang | excellent | 1 | 10919.23 | | Ding Xuexiang | poor | 4 | 3,555 | | Li Xi | good | 4 | 2938.53 |
Here is the complete returned collection for candidates (14 items).

scoreExplanations.actionability: Names Li Qiang as the pick and supports with 14-row table; buyer can act.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: No timestamp anywhere in the response body. Rubric rule 4 caps dataFreshness at 2.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Concrete 24h volumes, spreads, and liquidity tier per candidate.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Multi-signal screen across 14 candidates is paid-tier work.
scoreExplanations.completeness: User asked for 3 signals (24h volume, spread, 7-day drift) — the table only delivers 2 (volume + spread). 7-day drift is missing from every row.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: Values vary plausibly (Li Qiang volume 10,919 >> others at <3,500); internally consistent.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: analyze_market_liquidity was fanned out to 14 tickers but get_market_candlesticks was only called once (tc15, for Li Qiang) — the 7-day drift signal requires candlesticks for ALL 14 candidates.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner did not schedule per-candidate candlestick fetches as part of the 3-signal screen.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: Only one candlestick call (tc15) for KXXISUCCESSOR-45JAN01-LQIA — and its window is similar short-range, which means other candidates' drift is never computed.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: completeness.evaluations is empty despite the answer omitting the 7-day-drift dimension the user explicitly asked for.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc1..tc14 analyze_market_liquidity per ticker; tc15 single get_market_candlesticks call — the response claims 'positive 7-day YES-price drift' was part of the screen but the trace shows only Li Qiang had candles fetched at all.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Response presents Li Qiang as the 'smart-money plus momentum' pick but trace shows no candlestick data fetched for 13 of 14 candidates, so the momentum dimension is unsupported.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Solid liquidity-based screen mispresented as a 3-signal screen. Missing the momentum dimension the user asked for.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When a query requires N signals, the planner must fan out the corresponding data-fetch calls for EACH candidate — and completeness must verify that every declared signal has real data backing it.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: Same, but get_market_candlesticks should have been called for all 14 candidates
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: 14 candlestick calls, one per candidate ticker, with the same 7-day window
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: summary.toolCalls=16; only 1 candlestick call — 7-day drift for 13 candidates never computed.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: tc15 returned at least one candle with yesBid open=21 — limited history.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; the 3rd signal (drift) never flagged missing.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: On the fence — the 'Li Qiang' pick is probably correct on volume+spread, but the momentum dimension is asserted without supporting data.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Using only Kalshi data, find any candidate sub-market inside 'Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?' (KXNEXTUKPM-30) where best YES-ask + best NO-ask is strictly less than 100 cents (implied within-leg arbitrage). Report the leg, the YES-ask, the NO-ask, and the arbitrage edge in cents.
quotedExcerpts: Based on current Kalshi data (as of April 19, 2026), there are no sub-markets within the "Who will be the next Prime Minister of the UK?" event (KXNEXTUKPM-30) where the combined best YES-ask and best NO-ask is strictly less than 100 cents.
| Will Andrew Burnham be the next Prime Minister of United Kingdom? | 8.1¢ | 92.0¢ | -0.1¢ |

This result is based on a best-effort scan of the most liquid candidates. While the primary event listing was reviewed, detailed orderbooks were not fetched for every one of the 20 available sub-markets, so an exhaustive sweep of all illiquid legs was not completed.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Directly answers: no arbitrage found. Gives the closest 7 legs with exact edges — buyer can judge proximity.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Date-only ('as of April 19, 2026') with no per-leg timestamp.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Exact YES-ask, NO-ask, and edge in cents per leg.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-leg arbitrage scan with quantified edges is paid-tier.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Only 7 of 20 sub-markets scanned — acknowledged, but a full sweep was part of the ask.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: YES+NO values consistently exceed 100¢ (typical for Kalshi); no obvious red flags.

traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner chose to only scan 4 orderbooks initially, leaving 13+ illiquid legs unchecked — illiquid legs are exactly where YES+NO < 100¢ arbitrages live.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: Completeness stage didn't flag 'only 7/20 legs scanned' as missingParts, yet the response itself admits it.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0 get_event returns 20 markets. tc1..tc4 only hit 4 orderbooks. Response extrapolates to 7 candidates — that's using get_event inline quotes for the 3 additional rows without fetching their orderbooks.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: summary.toolCalls=5; only 4 orderbooks fetched out of 20 sub-markets despite a query that explicitly asks about 'any' sub-market.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Correctly concludes no arbitrage for scanned legs, but the honest caveat (13 legs unchecked) means the answer is not airtight.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: For per-event arbitrage scans, fetch orderbooks for ALL sub-markets in parallel (bounded concurrency) — partial scans cannot rule out arbitrage.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: Same tools but fan out get_market_orderbook across all 20 sub-markets in parallel
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: Same args, issued for all 20 sub-markets
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: tc0.result.markets.length === 20; tc1..tc4 orderbooks only → 16 sub-markets have no fetched YES/NO ask.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: tc1 KXNEXTUKPM-30-AR asks[0]={price:27,...}; bid-side summary used in response.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; 16 unscanned legs never surfaced as missingParts.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: On the fence — correct partial answer, but doesn't rule out arbitrage on unscanned illiquid legs.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Build a risk-diversified 5-market Kalshi shortlist of actively trading contracts priced between 15% and 40% YES where each market (a) has orderbook depth sufficient for a $2,500 YES position at <= 3 cents of slippage and (b) has 7-day cumulative volume >= $50K. The shortlist must include at least one market from Elections, one from Economics, and one from Science and Technology.
quotedExcerpts: I could not assemble a 5-market shortlist because the available data from Kalshi as of April 19, 2026, shows no active markets meeting your specific price, volume, and depth constraints within the required categories.
Elections: 0 active markets found. Economics: 0 active markets found. Science and Technology: 0 active markets found. Politics: 0 active markets found.
Relax Category Constraints: Other active categories currently include Climate and Weather (269 series), Companies (351 series), and Entertainment (2,358 series).

scoreExplanations.actionability: Zero shortlist rows delivered — buyer has nothing to trade.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Date-only; no per-market timestamps (because no markets).
scoreExplanations.specificity: Series counts (269 / 351 / 2,358) for alternative categories are the only concrete numbers; no tickers or prices.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: A free LLM could give similar 'relax your filters' advice.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Does not deliver the shortlist and doesn't fall back to an expanded-category partial draft.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: browse_category for Politics/Economics/Science returns 0 and the loop moves on to get_all_categories → browse_category Elections (0) → get_all_series Elections → browse_series KXCA1SPECIAL (0). Never calls analyze_market_liquidity on ANY ticker.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner did not model 'if category browse returns 0, descend via get_all_series + browse_series on every returned series ticker' — it only tried the first series (KXCA1SPECIAL).
traceAssessment.executionIssue: Loop gave up after one series-level browse returned empty, despite 20 Elections series being available per tc6.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: Completeness never raised a needsDifferentTools despite the trace showing 20 Elections series plus Economics series (CPIFOOD, KXOFAC, RATEHIKE from prior traces) that were never drilled into.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc6 get_all_series Elections returned KXCA1SPECIAL, KXALPRIMARY and more; tc7 only browsed KXCA1SPECIAL which returned empty, then loop ended. No additional series were tried.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: tc6 returned 20+ Elections series tickers but tc7 only browsed 1 before the loop synthesized a refusal.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Honest refusal, but the trace clearly had more series to try. Buyer gets no value and a mild insinuation that their filters are wrong.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Loop through ALL returned series tickers from get_all_series (with a cap, e.g., top 10 by frequency) before declaring a category empty.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: browse_series on each of the top-N series tickers returned by get_all_series, then analyze_market_liquidity on the resulting markets
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: Fan out browse_series for all returned series tickers (KXALPRIMARY, KXCA1SPECIAL, and 18 more)
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: tc6.result.series[].length > 1 but tc7 used only the first entry.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: No — the trace had 20+ series to drill into and only tried 1.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Across all sub-markets of 'What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?' (KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30), check whether the sum of best YES-ask implied probabilities equals 100 cents. If it does not, identify which outcome(s) are mispriced and quantify the total edge in cents.
quotedExcerpts: As of April 19, 2026, 02:30 UTC, the sum of the best YES-ask prices across the sub-markets for **'What will be the largest source of global primary energy consumption in 2030?' (KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30)** does not equal 100 cents.

The total sum of these prices is **106 cents**, representing a **6-cent overround**. This means the market is currently overvalued for a buyer seeking to cover all outcomes; there is no positive "edge" for buying the full YES basket.
| **OIL** | KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30-OIL | 55 | | **COAL** | KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30-COAL | 15 | | **GAS** | KXPRIMEENGCONSUMPTION-30-GAS | 14 |

scoreExplanations.actionability: Tells buyer the basket is 6¢ overround — clear 'don't arb-buy the basket' guidance.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Per-outcome tickers and ask prices for 10 outcomes, plus total.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: Cross-outcome basket-sum calculation is the exact thing a paid tool should do; free LLMs can't fetch per-sub-market asks.
scoreExplanations.completeness: Delivers overround magnitude + direction; weakness is it doesn't identify WHICH single outcomes are the 'mispriced' ones (user asked for per-outcome mispricing identification).
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: 55+15+14+4+4+3+3+3+3+3=107, but response totals 106 — minor arithmetic inconsistency worth noting. Values per outcome are plausible.

traceAssessment.executionIssue: Only one tool call (get_event) — no per-sub-market orderbook fetch to confirm the inline yesAsk prices are actually the current best asks (inline yesPrice may lag).
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: 'Identify which outcome(s) are mispriced' wasn't addressed; completeness stage didn't flag it as missingParts.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: tc0 get_event with withNestedMarkets returned the event's market list with inline yesPrice fields; the response used those as 'best yes ask' — defensible but a get_market_orderbook fan-out would have been more accurate.
traceAssessment.dataAccuracyRedFlags: Arithmetic inconsistency: listed values sum to 107 (55+15+14+4+4+3+3+3+3+3) but response reports 106 — one outcome's display value is off by 1.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: Sum of table values (107) ≠ reported total (106); likely rounding or a copied value not quite matching raw tool output.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: Strong answer with a minor arithmetic slip; also misses the per-outcome mispricing call-out.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: When summing probabilities, recompute in the synthesis layer from the raw numbers and flag any outcome whose ask deviates materially from its volume-weighted history as 'mispriced'.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: get_event + per-sub-market get_market_orderbook fan-out for authoritative best-asks
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: summary.toolCalls=1 but the question is a per-outcome analytic — 10 orderbook fetches would tighten the answer.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: tc0 markets[] contains 10+ sub-markets with inline yesPrice fields used as best-ask proxies.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; 'which outcomes are mispriced' not flagged.
diagnosticBrief.stageTimingBottleneck: executionMs=48924ms for a single tool call is high — suggests retries or timeout-adjacent latency inside the tool.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: Yes — buyer gets a clear overround answer; minor 1-cent arithmetic wobble is the only polish gap.

perQueryEvaluations.query: Identify the 3 Kalshi markets closing within the next 14 days with (a) cumulative 30-day volume > $100K and (b) the largest absolute gap between current YES midpoint and the 30-day implied-probability moving average. Report each market's current price, 30-day mean, gap in cents, and direction (rich vs cheap vs mean).
quotedExcerpts: Based on the data retrieved from the markets, there are currently no markets that meet the required criteria of having a cumulative 30-day volume greater than $100,000.
KXMVECROSSCATEGORY-S202602F99F7AF5C-F5AF47DF886 (Texas/Over 10.5 runs): Total volume of 37.
Additionally, the 30-day implied-probability moving averages required to calculate the price gaps were not available in the returned data.

scoreExplanations.actionability: Zero rows delivered — buyer cannot act.
scoreExplanations.dataFreshness: Date-only; no timestamps on example volumes.
scoreExplanations.specificity: Two example tickers with near-zero volumes are the only concrete data.
scoreExplanations.uniqueness: A free LLM with Kalshi docs could deduce 'try relaxing volume threshold'.
scoreExplanations.completeness: None of (current price / 30-day mean / gap / direction) delivered for any market.
scoreExplanations.dataAccuracy: No market-level numeric data to evaluate.

traceAssessment.selectionIssue: Looped get_markets with cursor paging across 13 calls, all returning low-volume sports multi-game composite markets. Never called get_market_candlesticks for the 30-day moving average, never used analyze_market_liquidity.
traceAssessment.planningIssue: Planner treated the problem as 'get_markets pagination' instead of 'get_markets + filter by closeTs within 14d + sort/filter by 30-day volume + candlestick fan-out for moving average'.
traceAssessment.executionIssue: executionMs=107926 (≈98% of async budget). 13 pagination calls exhausted the budget before any candlestick or volume-aggregation work could happen.
traceAssessment.completenessIssue: completeness.evaluations empty; the 30-day mean / gap / direction components (core to the question) never flagged as needing different tools.
traceAssessment.codeIssueDetail: All 13 toolCalls are get_markets with different cursors; same market shape each time (KXMVE* sports composites). No pivot to browse_category or get_event at any point.
traceAssessment.rootCauseEvidence: 13 consecutive get_markets pagination calls with no pivot, and no candlestick/MA call at all — budgetConsumedPct≈98% before synthesis.
traceAssessment.responseQualityNote: The pagination loop consumed essentially the full budget. Buyer pays premium for a refusal.
traceAssessment.improvementLever: Cap get_markets pagination at 3–5 pages; if criteria still unmet, pivot to browse_category with sortBy='volume_30d' or call discover_trending_markets on target categories before exhausting the budget.

diagnosticBrief.toolShouldHaveUsed: browse_category sortBy='close_time' or 'volume_30d' across target categories, plus per-market get_market_candlesticks for the 30-day moving average
diagnosticBrief.argsShouldHaveUsed: Targeted browse_category calls + candlestick fan-out with valid 2026 timestamps
diagnosticBrief.codeSnippet: stageTiming.executionMs=107926 / async budget ≈ 110000 → ~98% consumed on pagination alone.
diagnosticBrief.toolResultSample: tc0 markets[0].title = 'yes Boston,yes Oklahoma City,yes Detroit wins by over 9.5 points,...' — low-quality multi-leg composites.
diagnosticBrief.completenessVerdict: Empty evaluations[]; 30-day mean component never flagged as missing.

perQueryEvaluations.overallVerdict: No — near-budget-exhaust refusal; pagination loop was the wrong strategy.

topImprovementLevers: Fix the 30-day (and 7-day) historical-window timestamp computation — planner is anchoring to 2024 ts even though today is 2026; this single fix unblocks candlestick/momentum/drift queries (kalshi-011, kalshi-013, kalshi-018).

Teach iterative recovery to drill deeper when category browse returns 0 — after browse_category empty, fan out across get_all_series → browse_series on all returned tickers instead of giving up (kalshi-008, kalshi-016, kalshi-018).

Parallelize per-ticker orderbook / candlestick fan-out with bounded concurrency and cap get_markets pagination, so multi-sub-market screens don't burn budget and can complete full coverage (kalshi-006, kalshi-010, kalshi-014, kalshi-018).
